records.ety: [OE. ye, ya, e, a, AS. ge; akin to OFries. gē, iē, OS., D., OHG., G., Dan. & Sw. ja, Icel, jā, Goth. ja, jai, and probably to Gr. "h^ truly, verily. √188. Cf. Yes.]

defs: Yes; ay; a word expressing assent, or an affirmative, or an affirmative answer to a question, now superseded by yes. See Yes.
quotes: Let your communication be yea, yea; nay, nay.

defs: More than this; not only so, but; -- used to mark the addition of a more specific or more emphatic clause. Cf. Nay, adv., 2.
quotes: I therein do rejoice, yea, and will rejoice.
notes: ☞ Yea sometimes introduces a clause, with the sense of indeed, verily, truly. “Yea, hath God said, Ye shall not eat of every tree of the garden?”

defs: An affirmative vote; one who votes in the affirmative; as, a vote by yeas and nays.
notes: ☞ In the Scriptures, yea is used as a sign of certainty or stability. “All the promises of God in him are yea, and in him Amen.”
